regions.name: Quito & the Middle of the World
regions.stay_type: restored colonial mansion in the historic center

activities.category: UNESCO colonial center walk with local historian
Equatorial line and indigenous astronomy experience
Quito's emerging food scene dinner

regions.name: Amazon (Napo Province)
regions.stay_type: river-access eco-lodge with Kichwa community ties

activities.category: Canoe wildlife survey at dawn
Medicinal plant walk with community elder
Night hike for frogs and insects

regions.name: Otavalo & the northern highlands
regions.stay_type: hacienda on the Avenue of the Volcanoes

activities.category: Otavalo Saturday market (largest in the Americas)
Condor Park raptor encounter
Crater lake hike at Cuicocha

regions.name: Galápagos Islands (Santa Cruz base)
regions.stay_type: mid-range lodge in Puerto Ayora with day-trip access

activities.category: Tortoise reserve and Charles Darwin Research Station
Snorkeling with sea lions and marine iguanas
Island day trip by speedboat to Española or Bartolomé

common_mistakes: Treating the Galápagos as a quick add-on — three nights minimum for Santa Cruz-based visits, five for a liveaboard that actually covers multiple islands
Visiting Otavalo market on any day other than Saturday — the midweek market is a fraction of the real thing
Flying into the Amazon without malaria prevention discussion with your doctor ahead of time
Booking a Galápagos liveaboard without checking the route map — 'Galápagos cruise' covers everything from day-boats to proper multi-day vessels with very different wildlife access

regions.name: Quito
regions.stay_type: restored colonial hotel in the historic center

activities.category: Old Town UNESCO walking route
Teleferico cable car to Pichincha volcano flank

regions.name: Avenue of the Volcanoes
regions.stay_type: hacienda with views of multiple volcano peaks

activities.category: Cotopaxi National Park acclimatization hike
Quilotoa crater lake loop hike
Andean village market and weaving workshop
Ruta de las Cascadas cycling descent
Tungurahua volcano viewpoint at dusk
White-water rafting on the Pastaza

common_mistakes: Attempting Cotopaxi's upper slopes without proper acclimatization — altitude sickness is real and dangerous above 4,500m
Driving the Avenue of the Volcanoes in a rush — the haciendas along this route deserve at least two nights each to settle in
Visiting Baños during Carnival when the town becomes extremely crowded and rafting conditions are unpredictable

regions.name: Mindo (cloud forest)
regions.stay_type: cloud-forest lodge on the western Andes slope

activities.category: Dawn birdwatching — Mindo is one of the world's top birding corridors
Bean-to-bar cacao experience at a local chocolate producer
Butterfly farm and orchid reserve walk
Ruta de las Cascadas cycling descent past five waterfalls
White-water rafting on the Pastaza river
Tungurahua volcano viewpoint at dusk

regions.name: Amazonía (Tena & Suchipakari area)
regions.stay_type: Suchipakari Lodge — Kichwa-community eco-lodge on the Napo river

activities.category: Canoe wildlife survey at dawn with Kichwa naturalist guide
Medicinal plant and shamanic tradition walk with community elder
Cacao harvest and traditional chocolate preparation experience
Night hike — frogs, insects and Amazonian soundscape

common_mistakes: Visiting Mindo on a weekend when tourist traffic from Quito fills the main road — plan for a mid-week arrival for a quieter experience
Booking the Amazon leg for fewer than 3 nights — the Suchipakari area rewards slower travel; 4 nights is the minimum to truly settle in
Combining Baños with a Quito arrival on the same day — the altitude shift and long drive create a rough introduction
Flying into the Amazon without a yellow fever vaccination and malaria consultation — check health requirements well in advance

regions.name: Montañita & Olón
regions.stay_type: beach hostel or boutique hotel above the main strip

activities.category: Surf lessons on one of Ecuador's best point breaks
Montañita nightlife and artisan night market
Walk or bike to the quieter Olón beach and clifftop church

regions.name: Ayampe & Puerto López
regions.stay_type: beachfront eco-lodge in the quieter stretch north of Montañita

activities.category: Humpback whale watching (June–September — confirm seasonal dates)
Isla de la Plata day trip — snorkel with sea lions and blue-footed boobies
Kayak through the Ayampe mangrove estuary at low tide

common_mistakes: Confusing Montañita and Olón — they are back-to-back but very different in atmosphere; Montañita is party-forward, Olón is quieter and family-friendly
Missing whale season (June–September) if whale watching is a priority — outside this window Puerto López has limited appeal
Arriving on the coast from Quito in a single long day — break it with a Mindo overnight and arrive rested
Underestimating Pacific rip currents — swim only at flagged sections and always ask locals before entering unfamiliar breaks
